title: Complete G2 operations with exact drills and atomic real-history shadow backfill
context: The ten-policy threshold alone left G2 operationally incomplete: no executable category drills, no exact rehearsal evidence source, and only same-HEAD shadow collection. Full implementation must accumulate distinct real states without inflating retries or treating pre-policy commits as violations.
decision: Add seven plan-bound hash-only operational drills across core, CLI, and client-neutral MCP; add CLI-only atomic first-parent shadow backfill that preflights the complete policy/commit matrix, writes nothing on unknown/error, deduplicates exact identities, and excludes executable-policy commits predating the fixing commit from precision scoring while retaining raw audit records. Drills never self-attest, readiness never signs off, and all blocking remains disabled.
consequences: Operators can produce exact evidence hashes for every required runbook rehearsal without granting authority.
Real historical commits can establish bounded shadow baselines without synthetic commits or partial batches.
Pre-policy failures remain auditable but cannot pollute operational precision denominators.
alternatives_rejected: Lower the 20-observation threshold or repeat one HEAD — rejected because it manufactures readiness; revisit if a future human-approved G2 protocol preregisters a different statistically justified bound.

Persist successful cells before the full matrix finishes — rejected because one late evaluator error would leave a biased partial baseline; revisit if the repository gains an explicit transactional batch ledger with recovery semantics.

Auto-record passed rehearsals or G2 signoff from drill success — rejected because machine diagnostics cannot impersonate human evidence or authority; revisit only if the human-actor Constitution contract is explicitly superseded.
